portfolio: Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications and the Arts
executive_summary: The NLA is a corporate Commonwealth entity and cultural/collecting institution stewarding one of the world's largest collections, with its national digital platform Trove at the centre of its mission. This year's plan is dominated by infrastructure modernisation: a five-year roadmap to replace Trove's end-of-life software (including its metadata engine), cyber-security uplift, major heritage-building works on the Parkes building, and an extension of the Hume Repository to address long-term collection storage. The plan openly flags funding-sustainability risk, end-of-life core IT systems, labour-market competition for specialist skills, and new (baseline-to-be-set) performance measures. Notably, the NLA is reducing outsourcing under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework, which is a headwind for advisory spend, so opportunities should be proportionate to a modest budget and concentrated where the Library lacks internal capacity (digital build, costing/business cases, assurance). ANAO follow-on audit findings (storage strategy, deselection/disposal policy, business continuity, LMS security) and a reported staff underpayment add concrete, demand-creating pressures.

strategic_priorities.title: Modernise and stabilise Trove
strategic_priorities.description: Execute a five-year implementation roadmap to replace Trove's end-of-life software, stabilise and secure the platform, and replace the underlying metadata engine to improve efficiency and capacity.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We are executing a five-year implementation roadmap to replace Trove's end-of-life software elements, and to stabilise and secure the platform... Modernise the underlying infrastructure to improve Trove's efficiency and capacity, including replacement of the metadata engine.

strategic_priorities.title: Major building works and long-term collection storage
strategic_priorities.description: Progress major works and preventative maintenance on the heritage-listed Parkes building, construct the Hume Repository extension, and plan for longer-term storage needs while remaining open to the public.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Progress the major building works and preventative maintenance program... Construct the extension to the Hume Repository and continue to plan for longer-term storage needs.

strategic_priorities.title: Digital, data and cyber capability uplift
strategic_priorities.description: Execute the Digital Strategy to ensure suitable digital infrastructure, uplift cyber security, adopt cloud/SaaS in line with government priorities, deliver a more accessible website and collection search, and operationalise handwritten text recognition/transcription.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Execute the National Library's Digital Strategy to ensure ongoing suitability of digital infrastructure, uplift cyber security, responsibly adopt technology...

strategic_priorities.title: Funding sustainability and demonstrating return on investment
strategic_priorities.description: Secure a sustainable funding and resource model to meet the institutional mandate, maintain the heritage building, and sustain Trove to world-leading standards; continue to advocate for future funding needs to government.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: The National Library does not have a sustainable funding and resource model to meet its institutional mandate... The National Library will continue to advocate for future funding needs through reporting to government.

strategic_priorities.title: Reshape workforce and reduce reliance on external labour
strategic_priorities.description: Strengthen capability via the Strategic People Pathway, reduce outsourcing of core (initially information/knowledge management) work under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework, and embed the new Enterprise Agreement.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: In 2024-25, the National Library will reduce outsourcing of core work in line with the Government's APS Strategic Commissioning Framework.

strategic_priorities.title: Refresh the performance framework
strategic_priorities.description: Introduce new effectiveness measures (digital and onsite visitor satisfaction surveys with baselines to be set) and retire several output/service-charter measures to improve the 'clear read' across PBS, Corporate Plan and Annual Report.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: This new performance measure will provide valuable feedback... Baseline to be set.

reform_and_change_drivers: National Cultural Policy (Revive) alignment shaping collecting, engagement and infrastructure priorities
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework requiring reduced outsourcing of core work
Generative AI rapidly altering the operating context for a custodian of large-scale digital content
Government cloud/SaaS priorities driving migration of enterprise services
2023 funding commitment creating public expectation that building and service upgrades will be delivered
New Enterprise Agreement (effective April 2024) requiring refresh of supporting policies
Physical collection growth of ~2.5km per year intensifying storage pressure
challenges_and_risks: Funding/resource model may be insufficient to sustain the mandate, building and Trove over the medium-long term
Core IT systems at end of life; rising digital infrastructure, supplier, project and contractor costs
Increasing cyber-security threats to systems and data, against a stated low risk appetite for confidentiality/integrity/availability
Labour-market competition for specialist skills and reliance on hard-to-retain expertise
Managing multiple building projects within a functioning, heritage-listed public facility
Long-term collection storage unresolved as current arrangements approach end of life
New satisfaction measures lack baselines, leaving effectiveness reporting immature in 2024-25

news_and_controversies.summary: Reporting referenced a finding that the NLA underpaid staff by approximately $250,000, framed by the Fair Work Ombudsman as a warning to public employers.
news_and_controversies.implication: Payroll/award-compliance remediation and HR systems assurance are plausible near-term needs and a credible workforce/governance entry point.

news_and_controversies.summary: Coverage of the ANAO follow-on audit reported the collections face significant future risks without a funded long-term storage solution (leasing arrangements ending around 2025), with two draft preservation policies unfinalised since 2009 and library-management-system security shortcomings.
news_and_controversies.implication: Capital/storage business-case development, preservation policy finalisation and IT/records security remediation are concrete advisory opportunities tied to a publicly stated risk.

anao_findings.summary: Auditor-General Report No. 29 of 2020-21 'Management of the National Collections — Follow-on' found NLA governance and collection management 'largely effective' but flagged no plan for removing items from collections, unresolved long-term storage, preservation recording not integrated with the collection-management system, and sub-standard library-management-system security. Two recommendations to the NLA (update business continuity plan; develop a deselection/disposal policy) were agreed.
anao_findings.implication: Audit-driven, agreed recommendations (deselection/disposal policy, business continuity, storage strategy, system security) are ready-made, defensible workstreams for assurance, governance and digital remediation support.

parliamentary_matters.summary: ANAO Report No. 29 (2020-21) was tabled in Parliament and recorded in ParlInfo, including the Library's summary response noting 'significant future risks' around storage funding.
parliamentary_matters.implication: The Library's own tabled words on funding/storage risk provide useful framing for a business-case or budget-bid advisory pitch.

opportunity_areas.area: Trove platform modernisation and metadata engine replacement
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Replace Trove's end-of-life software and metadata engine over a five-year roadmap while stabilising and securing the platform.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery/solution-design and build support for the metadata-engine replacement and platform re-platforming, including systems/process analysis, integration and service transition — scoped to discrete roadmap phases rather than a single large build.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names a specific multi-year roadmap, a metadata-engine replacement and an end-of-life-software problem; Trove is the Library's flagship asset. The firm can build and implement, not only advise. Confidence tempered by funding constraints and the outsourcing-reduction agenda.

opportunity_areas.area: Long-term collection storage business case (Hume Repository / Parkes)
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Secure a funded long-term storage solution and justify capital investment amid growth of ~2.5km of physical collection per year and leasing arrangements ending around 2025.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial-sustainability and capital business-case engagement: options analysis, costing and a budget-bid-ready business case for long-term storage, supporting the Library's funding advocacy to government.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan flags an unsustainable funding/resource model and unresolved long-term storage; ANAO and media reporting both name storage funding as a significant future risk, strengthening the case.

opportunity_areas.area: Audit-recommendation implementation: deselection/disposal policy and business continuity
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Close agreed ANAO recommendations to develop a deselection/disposal policy and update the business continuity plan, and finalise long-standing draft preservation policies.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A short policy/governance engagement to draft the deselection/disposal policy, refresh the business continuity plan and finalise preservation policies, aligned to the Archives Act and the Library's information-governance framework.
opportunity_areas.rationale: ANAO Report No. 29 made two agreed recommendations and media reporting noted unfinalised preservation policies since 2009 — concrete, audit-driven, defensible scope for a small advisory team.

opportunity_areas.area: Cyber-security uplift and system security assurance
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Uplift cyber security and address library-management-system security shortcomings, with a stated low risk appetite for system confidentiality, integrity and availability.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A security assessment and assurance review of Trove and the library-management system, with a prioritised remediation roadmap aligned to the Library's Cyber Security Strategy and PSPF obligations.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to cyber uplift and cites increasing threats; ANAO specifically found LMS security 'not up to standard', creating an evidenced demand signal.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Operationalise handwritten text recognition/transcription and implement the Digitisation Strategy to increase accessible digitised content.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Implementation support for handwritten text recognition and transcription pipelines, plus an information-management/AI-readiness assessment to safely adopt generative AI against Trove's deep digital materials.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names operationalising transcription via enhanced handwritten text recognition and signals measured AI adoption; this is data/information work proportionate to the Library's scale.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Set baselines for new digital and onsite visitor-satisfaction measures and ensure a 'clear read' across PBS, Corporate Plan and Annual Report.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A PGPA-aligned performance-measure review to design the new satisfaction measures, set baselines and methodology, and confirm clear-read alignment — a short advisory piece.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan introduces new effectiveness measures with 'baseline to be set' and removed several measures, signalling a framework in transition. Some of this may be done in-house given outsourcing reduction, so confidence is moderate.

opportunity_areas.area: Payroll/award compliance and EA policy refresh
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Remediate reported staff underpayment and refresh the many supporting policies affected by the 2024-2027 Enterprise Agreement.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A workforce/HR engagement to review award and EA compliance, remediation assurance, and a refresh of supporting people policies post-EA.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Media reporting cited an ~$250k underpayment and the plan notes a major EA driving policy refresh. Tempered by the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework reducing reliance on external workforce providers.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the CIO / digital infrastructure leadership with a phased delivery proposition for the Trove metadata-engine replacement, leading with build-and-implement credentials and discrete roadmap phases.
recommended_actions.rationale: This is the largest, time-bound, named opportunity in the plan and plays to the firm's ability to build, not just advise — differentiating it from pure advisers.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a tailored capability statement and outreach to the CFO / corporate group offering a long-term storage business case and financial-sustainability review, citing the ANAO follow-on audit and the Library's own tabled funding-risk statement.
recommended_actions.rationale: Storage funding is an explicit strategic risk reinforced by ANAO and Parliament; a budget-bid-ready business case directly serves the Library's stated advocacy need.

recommended_actions.action: Offer the Audit and Enterprise Risk Committee / Director-General's office a packaged 'audit-recommendation closure' engagement covering the deselection/disposal policy, business continuity update and preservation-policy finalisation.
recommended_actions.rationale: Agreed ANAO recommendations and long-unfinalised policies are low-political-risk, defensible scope that the committee is accountable for closing.

recommended_actions.action: Pitch a focused cyber-security assessment of Trove and the library-management system to the CIO and CISO function, referencing the ANAO LMS-security finding.
recommended_actions.rationale: Cyber uplift is a stated capability action with a specific audit-evidenced weakness and a low risk appetite, making it an easy-to-justify engagement.

recommended_actions.action: Watch for procurement signals around the Trove roadmap, Hume Repository works and digital infrastructure, and confirm panel positioning (management consulting and digital services) before any approach to market.
recommended_actions.rationale: Multi-year reform programs will generate staged procurements; early positioning on the right panels is essential given the firm sells almost exclusively to Commonwealth entities.

engagement_targets: Director-General and Executive (delegated owners of the Risk Management Framework and strategic priorities)
Chief Information Officer / digital infrastructure leadership (Trove, Digital Strategy, cyber, cloud migration)
Chief Financial Officer / corporate group (funding sustainability, storage business case, capital works)
Audit and Enterprise Risk Committee (ANAO recommendation closure, business continuity, security)
Head of collections / preservation (deselection/disposal and preservation policies, storage planning)
HR / People function (EA policy refresh, payroll compliance, Strategic People Pathway)
talking_points: Trove's five-year roadmap and metadata-engine replacement need a delivery partner that can build and integrate, not just advise — we can scope discrete, fundable phases that de-risk an end-of-life platform.
Your tabled funding/storage risk and the ANAO follow-on findings point to a budget-bid-ready capital business case for long-term storage — we can build the options analysis and costing.
We can package the closure of your agreed ANAO recommendations — deselection/disposal policy and business continuity — alongside finalising the long-standing draft preservation policies.

With a stated low risk appetite for system integrity and an ANAO finding on library-management-system security, a targeted cyber assessment of Trove and the LMS gives you an evidenced remediation roadmap.
Your new visitor-satisfaction measures need baselines and clear-read alignment — a short PGPA-aligned performance review will firm them up before annual reporting.
We understand the Strategic Commissioning Framework — our offerings are scoped to where you genuinely lack internal capacity, not to displace core work you're bringing in-house.
caveats: This is a relatively thin, mission-focused plan from a small-budget cultural institution, so opportunities must be right-sized and several map to short advisory or discrete build phases rather than large programs. A material headwind is the Library's explicit commitment to reduce outsourcing under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework, which may suppress advisory demand — opportunities are strongest where the Library lacks internal capacity (digital build, costing/business cases, cyber assurance). External research was limited by a search-quota cap: the Trove-funding story and recent Senate Estimates/QON were not surfaced, the key ANAO audit is from 2020-21 (just outside the preferred window), and the underpayment figure is single-sourced. Funding constraints make timing and procurement appetite uncertain.
